summarization.a: The figure presents multiple X-ray diffraction spectra comparing lithium-intercalated manganese oxide samples, highlighting changes in diffraction peaks associated with the formation of lithiated phases and the reduction of β-MnO₂ features.

data_extraction.b:
| Intensity (arb. units) | °20 |
| --- | --- |
| Lithiated phase | 15 |
| 50cLi-MnO2 | 20 |
| 10cLi-MnO2 | 25 |
| β-MnO2 (100) | 30 |
| MnO2 | 35 |
| β-MnO2 (200) | 40 |
| β-MnO2 (400) | 45 |

a.question: Which crystalline phase becomes more evident after lithiation according to the diffraction patterns?
Does the figure indicate that lithiation alters the crystal structure of manganese oxide?

b.question: How do the X-ray diffraction patterns change as lithium content increases across the samples?
b.answer: As lithium content increases, diffraction peaks associated with Li₁₋ₓMn₂O₄ become more prominent, while peaks characteristic of β-MnO₂ decrease in intensity, indicating a progressive phase transformation driven by lithiation.

b.question: What application-relevant insights can be inferred from the observed phase evolution?
b.answer: Confirmation of lithium intercalation into manganese oxide, Structural transformation toward spinel-type phases, Relevance for lithium-ion battery electrode performance
